items.definition_en: An ATM is a machine where you can get cash from your bank account.
items.example_en: I used the ATM to take out money.

items.definition_en: A debit card is a card you use to pay with money from your bank account.
items.example_en: He paid for lunch with his debit card.

items.definition_en: A credit card is a card that lets you borrow money to pay for things.
items.example_en: She bought a new jacket with her credit card.

items.definition_en: A bank account is where your money is kept in a bank.
items.example_en: He opened a new bank account at the local branch.

items.definition_en: A checkbook is a book of checks you use to pay people.
items.example_en: She carried her checkbook in her purse.

items.definition_en: A PIN is a secret number for using your card or account.
items.example_en: He always remembers his PIN at the ATM.

items.definition_en: A deposit slip is a form to put money into your bank account.
items.example_en: She filled out a deposit slip before giving her cash to the teller.

items.definition_en: A withdrawal slip is a piece of paper for taking money out of your account.
items.example_en: He used a withdrawal slip to get cash at the bank.

items.definition_en: A safe deposit box is a locked box in a bank for keeping valuables safe.
items.example_en: They keep their jewelry in a safe deposit box.

items.definition_en: A check is a printed paper you sign to pay money from your bank.
items.example_en: He wrote a check to pay the rent.

items.definition_en: A savings book is a small book that records money in your savings account.
items.example_en: Her savings book shows all her deposits.

items.definition_en: A receipt is a piece of paper that shows you paid for something.
items.example_en: Don’t forget to keep your receipt from the ATM.

items.definition_en: A cash card is a card you use at a bank machine to get money.
items.example_en: He used his cash card at the bank.

items.definition_en: A statement is a paper that shows all the money in your account.
items.example_en: She checked her statement online.

items.definition_en: A bankbook is a small book with a record of your savings at the bank.
items.example_en: He updated his bankbook at the counter.

items.definition_en: A deposit box is a secure box at the bank to store important things.
items.example_en: They keep their documents in a deposit box.

items.definition_en: A bank card is a card from your bank to use for transactions.
items.example_en: She lost her bank card and called the bank.

items.definition_en: A signature card is a card at the bank with your signature for identification.
items.example_en: He signed a signature card to open the account.

items.definition_en: An envelope is a paper cover for holding money or important papers.
items.example_en: He put the cash in an envelope at the bank.

items.definition_en: A check register is a book for keeping track of checks and deposits.
items.example_en: She wrote the payment in her check register.
